pr.title: ftCaptain match, ftPopo proper splits & matches
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched Captain Falcon ftCa_SpecialLwEndAir_Coll by replacing an inline asm block with a simple C wrapper to ftCa_SpecialLwEnd_Coll, and reorganized Popo/Ice Climbers code into more accurate SpecialN and SpecialS splits. The PR moved SpecialN code out of ftPp_Init.c into a new ftPp_SpecialN.c/.h, added a new ftPp_SpecialS.c with matched helper routines, fixed obvious misnamed SpecialN references that actually belonged to SpecialS, and updated build/includes/data references accordingly.
what_changed: Replaced the MUST_MATCH inline asm implementation of ftCa_SpecialLwEndAir_Coll with C; the matched C body is just ftCa_SpecialLwEnd_Coll(gobj), with the parameter typed as Fighter_GObj*.

Created src/melee/ft/chara/ftPopo/ftPp_SpecialN.c and moved Popo neutral special routines out of ftPp_Init.c, including ftPp_SpecialN_Enter, ftPp_SpecialAirN_Enter, Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll functions, and ftPp_SpecialN_8011F500.
Created ftPp_SpecialN.h and removed the SpecialN declarations from ftPp_Init.h, making the header split match the source split.
Created src/melee/ft/chara/ftPopo/ftPp_SpecialS.c and added SpecialS helpers including ftPp_SpecialS_8011F68C and ftPp_SpecialS_8011F6FC.
Updated ftPp_Init_8011F060 to call ftPp_SpecialS_8011F68C instead of the prior ftPp_SpecialN_8011F68C name, reflecting the corrected split/function ownership.
Updated asm/melee/ft/chara/ftNn_Init.s call labels in SpecialS collision code from ftPp_SpecialN_8011F6FC to ftPp_SpecialS_8011F6FC.
Removed several sdata2 float/double symbols from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPp_SpecialS.s after the split/matches made them owned elsewhere or expressible from C.
Added ftPp_SpecialN.c.o and ftPp_SpecialS.c.o to obj_files.mk so the new C split files participate in the build.
Updated ftNana init and ftdata includes to pull in ftPp_SpecialN.h alongside the existing Popo headers.
smart_moves: Recognized that ftCa_SpecialLwEndAir_Coll was behaviorally identical to the existing ground/end collision helper and matched it by delegating instead of duplicating a fragile branch tree.
Separated Popo SpecialN code from Init rather than continuing to grow ftPp_Init.c, improving split fidelity and making future neutral-special work easier to localize.
Corrected function ownership by renaming/calling SpecialS-prefixed helpers where the surrounding split and callsites showed they were not SpecialN routines.
Used header movement as part of the split, not just source movement, so declarations now advertise the same module boundaries as the object split.
Kept partially unmatched work isolated: ftPp_SpecialN_8011F500 remained inline asm under MWERKS_GEKKO while nearby simpler routines were converted to C.
decomp_lessons: When two action-state collision callbacks compile to the same behavior, a direct wrapper call can be the cleanest matching solution and avoids preserving duplicated decompiled control flow.
Bad early names often come from temporary split placement; re-check function names against neighboring callsites and action groups before cementing them in headers.
Object-file splits should be reflected in obj_files.mk, source layout, and headers together; moving only the C code leaves future matches harder to reason about.
If a helper is called by SpecialS collision code and reset/init code in a SpecialS context, prefer correcting the prefix to SpecialS rather than keeping a misleading SpecialN address name.
Small constants such as 0.0f, 1.0f, and -10.0 can sometimes be expressed directly in C after a split, reducing dependence on ad hoc extern sdata labels.
naming_conventions: Popo neutral-special functions use the ftPp_SpecialN_* prefix and live in ftPp_SpecialN.c/.h.
Popo side-special functions and helpers use the ftPp_SpecialS_* prefix and live in ftPp_SpecialS.c/.h.
Address-suffixed names remain acceptable for unknown helpers, but the action prefix should still match the owning split, e.g. ftPp_SpecialS_8011F6FC rather than ftPp_SpecialN_8011F6FC.
Header guards in the new ftPp_SpecialN.h use _FTPP_SPECIALN_, while existing ftPp_SpecialS.h retained its older GALE01-style guard.
The PR provides evidence that obvious function-name corrections based on splits were accepted without review discussion.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed an inline asm Captain Falcon collision callback by proving the air-end variant can call the existing ftCa_SpecialLwEnd_Coll implementation.
Converted several Popo SpecialN routines to C while leaving the larger ftPp_SpecialN_8011F500 function as MWERKS_GEKKO inline asm, allowing incremental progress.
Used explicit unused-stack padding under MUST_MATCH in ftPp_SpecialAirN_Enter and ftPp_SpecialAirN_Coll to preserve stack layout where needed.
Adjusted asm labels/calls in ftNn_Init.s to match corrected symbol names without changing instruction encodings.
Reduced asm-owned sdata symbols in ftPp_SpecialS.s after C code began using literals or relocated constants.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body states the intent directly: match ftCa_SpecialLwEndAir_Coll, fix Popo splits and obviously incorrect function names, and match several Popo functions/data.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully decompile ftPp_SpecialN_8011F500 after this split.
Check whether ftPp_SpecialS_8011F720 and ftPp_SpecialS_8011F964 were subsequently matched or renamed.
Look for other Falcon collision callbacks that can be matched by delegating to an existing ground/air shared helper.
Audit Popo/Nana shared asm labels for remaining SpecialN vs SpecialS prefix mistakes.
